models.best_use_cases: Tabular prediction with mixed data types; churn, credit risk, demand forecasting, uplift; medium-to-large datasets where accuracy and robustness matter.
models.data_requirements: Tabular CSV/Excel; handles numeric and categorical features; missing values tolerated; benefits from sensible scaling of numeric features and ordinal/time encoding for forecasting.
models.strengths: High predictive accuracy; robust to outliers and mixed feature types; handles non-linearities and interactions; good generalization with proper regularization.
models.weaknesses: Slower training than linear models; more hyperparameters to tune; can overfit on small/noisy data; less suited for pure unsupervised tasks.
models.explainability_method: SHAP (TreeSHAP), feature importance, partial dependence plots

models.best_use_cases: Stable baseline for tabular classification/regression; noisy or high-cardinality categorical data; quick prototyping and robustness checks.
models.data_requirements: Tabular CSV/Excel; numeric and categorical features; handles missing values via imputation; little to no scaling required.
models.strengths: Robust to noise and overfitting via bagging; parallelizable training; provides reliable feature importances; works well out-of-the-box.
models.weaknesses: Larger model size and memory footprint; can be biased toward high-cardinality categorical features; less accurate than tuned gradient boosting on many tasks.

models.best_use_cases: Interpretable risk scoring, propensity modeling, survey response prediction; settings requiring coefficient-level explanations and statistical inference.
models.data_requirements: Tabular CSV/Excel; numeric features preferred or properly encoded categoricals; benefits from scaling and handling multicollinearity; missing values should be imputed.
models.strengths: Highly interpretable coefficients; fast training and inference; probabilistic outputs; strong baseline and regularization options (L1/L2).
models.weaknesses: Limited to linear decision boundaries; performance can lag on complex non-linear patterns; sensitive to feature scaling and collinearity.
models.explainability_method: Coefficient analysis, odds ratios, standardized coefficients, partial dependence

models.best_use_cases: Continuous outcome modeling with interpretability; pricing, forecasting with linear trends, causal factor analysis in surveys/operations.
models.data_requirements: Tabular CSV/Excel; numeric features; scaling recommended; handle multicollinearity and outliers; missing values should be addressed.
models.strengths: Transparent, fast, and well-understood; provides confidence intervals and p-values for inference; simple to deploy and audit.
models.weaknesses: Assumes linearity and homoscedasticity; sensitive to outliers and collinearity; poor fit for complex non-linear relationships.
models.explainability_method: Coefficient analysis, standardized coefficients, residual diagnostics, partial dependence

models.best_use_cases: High-performance tabular prediction; competitions and production systems needing accuracy and regularization; churn, LTV, demand/sales forecasting.
models.data_requirements: Tabular CSV/Excel; numeric and encoded categorical features; handles missing values natively; time-aware splits required for forecasting.
models.strengths: State-of-the-art accuracy with regularization; efficient handling of missing values; scalable with tree pruning and weighted quantile sketch.
models.weaknesses: More tuning complexity; can overfit without careful regularization; higher memory/CPU during training than simpler models.
models.explainability_method: SHAP (TreeSHAP), feature importance, gain-based importance, partial dependence

models.best_use_cases: Large tabular datasets with speed and memory constraints; high-cardinality categoricals; real-time scoring pipelines; forecasting with many series.
models.data_requirements: Tabular CSV/Excel; efficient with categoricals (native support); handles missing values; benefits from time-based validation for forecasting.
models.strengths: Fast training and low memory usage; good accuracy; handles categoricals well; leaf-wise growth for deeper trees when beneficial.
models.weaknesses: Can overfit on small datasets without tuning; leaf-wise growth may increase complexity; requires care with learning rate and num_leaves.

models.best_use_cases: Datasets with many categorical features; minimal preprocessing pipelines; marketing response, churn, and price modeling with mixed data.
models.data_requirements: Tabular CSV/Excel; native handling of categoricals; handles missing values; time-aware splits for forecasting; prefers ordered boosting to reduce overfit.
models.strengths: Strong out-of-the-box performance with categoricals; reduces target leakage via ordered boosting; robust to default parameters.
models.weaknesses: Slower training than LightGBM on very large data; larger model size; may require more memory.
models.explainability_method: SHAP (TreeSHAP), feature importance, object importance, partial dependence

models.best_use_cases: Customer segmentation, operational grouping, survey response clustering; exploratory profiling and cohort creation.
models.data_requirements: Tabular CSV/Excel; numeric features scaled appropriately; choose k and distance metric; sensitive to outliers and scale.
models.strengths: Simple, fast, and scalable; easy to implement and interpret clusters via centroids; widely supported in tooling.
models.weaknesses: Assumes spherical clusters and equal variance; sensitive to initialization and outliers; requires pre-specifying k.
models.explainability_method: Cluster centroids, silhouette analysis, PCA/t-SNE projection, feature contributions to centroids

models.best_use_cases: Anomaly/outlier detection and density-based segmentation; operational logs, survey open-ended behavior clusters with variable density.
models.data_requirements: Tabular CSV/Excel; numeric features; careful choice of eps and min_samples; scaling strongly affects results.
models.strengths: Finds arbitrary-shaped clusters; robust to outliers/noise; does not require specifying number of clusters.
models.weaknesses: Sensitive to eps and min_samples; struggles with varying densities; high-dimensional data degrades distance metrics.
models.explainability_method: Cluster membership, core/boundary/noise labels, reachability plots, PCA/t-SNE visualization

models.best_use_cases: Survey and taxonomy building; nested segmentation; dendrogram-driven decisions in education/business cohorts.
models.data_requirements: Tabular CSV/Excel; numeric features; linkage and distance metric selection; scaling recommended; can be memory-intensive.
models.strengths: Produces dendrogram for multi-level interpretation; no need to pre-specify k; intuitive for nested groupings.
models.weaknesses: Computationally heavy for large datasets; sensitive to noise and scale; merging decisions are global (depending on linkage).
models.explainability_method: Dendrogram, cophenetic correlation, cluster cuts at chosen height, feature contributions via aggregated cluster stats

models.best_use_cases: Soft clustering and probabilistic segment assignment; survey latent classes; operational states with uncertainty estimates.
models.data_requirements: Tabular CSV/Excel; numeric features; assumes approximate Gaussian clusters; scaling recommended; choose number of components.
models.strengths: Provides cluster probabilities; flexible covariance structures; principled model selection via BIC/AIC.
models.weaknesses: Assumes Gaussian-like clusters; sensitive to initialization; can struggle with non-elliptical shapes.
models.explainability_method: Component means/covariances, posterior probabilities, BIC/AIC, visualization via PCA/t-SNE

models.best_use_cases: Univariate time series with trend/seasonality; demand, enrollment, revenue forecasting; classical econometric settings.
models.data_requirements: Regular time index; numeric target; stationarity or differencing applied; exogenous variables optional (ARIMAX).
models.strengths: Well-understood statistical foundation; provides confidence intervals; interpretable parameters (AR/MA terms).
models.weaknesses: Primarily univariate and linear; manual parameter selection can be tedious; less flexible for complex multivariate patterns.
models.explainability_method: Parameter estimates, residual diagnostics, ACF/PACF plots, forecast decomposition

models.best_use_cases: Business time series with strong seasonality/holidays; sales, enrollment, operations forecasting with interpretable components.
models.data_requirements: Tabular CSV/Excel with ds (datetime) and y (numeric); optional regressors; handles missing dates and outliers gracefully.
models.strengths: Automatic seasonality/holiday modeling; robust to missing data and outliers; interpretable trend/seasonality components.
models.weaknesses: Additive model by default; may underfit complex non-linear interactions; primarily univariate with optional regressors.
models.explainability_method: Component plots (trend, seasonality, holidays), changepoints, regressor coefficients, cross-validation performance

models.best_use_cases: Seasonal univariate time series; periodic education/business cycles; inventory, staffing, and enrollment forecasts.
models.data_requirements: Regular time index; numeric target; seasonal period specified; stationarity or differencing (including seasonal differencing).
models.strengths: Explicit seasonal modeling; statistical rigor and confidence intervals; interpretable seasonal AR/MA terms.
models.weaknesses: Parameter space larger and more complex; primarily univariate; manual tuning can be time-consuming.
models.explainability_method: Parameter estimates, residual diagnostics, seasonal decomposition, ACF/PACF plots

models.best_use_cases: Survey and assessment dimensionality reduction; latent trait extraction; feature grouping and multicollinearity reduction.
models.data_requirements: Tabular CSV/Excel; numeric indicators; sufficient sample size; scaling recommended; choose number of factors.
models.strengths: Explicit latent factor model; provides loadings and communalities; useful for questionnaire design and construct validation.
models.weaknesses: Assumes linear relationships and Gaussian factors; rotation choices affect interpretation; requires care in selecting factor count.
models.explainability_method: Factor loadings, scree plots, variance explained, rotation matrices (varimax/promax), correlation with factors

models.best_use_cases: Dimensionality reduction and decorrelation; preprocessing for modeling; survey/assessment item compression.
models.data_requirements: Tabular CSV/Excel; numeric features; scaling essential; choose number of components or variance threshold.
models.strengths: Fast and deterministic; orthogonal components; clear variance-based selection; widely applicable.
models.weaknesses: Linear only; components may be hard to interpret in original features; does not model latent constructs explicitly like factor analysis.
models.explainability_method: Component loadings, explained variance ratios, biplots, correlation with original features
